raw_text:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
For November 2024, total actual spend was **$62,100** against a budget of **$33,000**, producing an unfavourable variance of **+$29,100 (+88.2%)**. The overage is concentrated almost entirely in a single line item: **Professional Fees**, which alone accounts for $27,500 of the total variance. This reflects a large, CFO-approved invoice from outside counsel (Whitmore & Associates) in connection with an active IP dispute defence against a patent infringement claim filed by Vantec Corp. The remaining $1,200 overage in **Legal & Compliance** is attributable to a CFO-approved flat-fee invoice for employment agreements related to two VP-level hires. All other line items are within normal tolerances. Of note, the IP matter carries estimated residual exposure of **$8K–$18K**, expected to fall in Q1 2025 and not currently budgeted; Finance should consider provisioning for this forward cost.

LINE COMMENTARY

Professional Fees | +$27,500 (+183.3%) | Budget: $15,000 | Actual: $42,500
The $27,500 unfavourable variance is driven by a single, CFO-approved invoice from **Whitmore & Associates** (Matter WA-2024-IP-441), billed for IP dispute advisory work performed October–November 2024 in preparation for USPTO proceedings. The underlying matter relates to a patent infringement claim filed by **Vantec Corp.** in September 2024.
Key context:
- Whitmore & Associates was engaged in August 2024 to lead the defence. Total estimated matter cost is **$45K–$55K** over approximately six months.
- A first invoice of **$12K** was absorbed within budget in September/October 2024.
- The November invoice of **$27,500** is the second billing and accounts for the full November overage.
- Cumulative matter spend to date is approximately **$39.5K** against a ~$50K estimate per Slack context (noting this estimate range extends to $55K per General Counsel's email).
- Remaining exposure of approximately **$8K–$18K** is expected to fall in **Q1 2025**, coinciding with the start of USPTO proceedings. Outside counsel assesses a strong defence position.
The November spend itself is reconciled against the invoice and CFO approval. However, residual Q1 2025 exposure is not yet reflected in forward budgets and **requires provisioning or budget re-forecasting**. Current evidence indicates remaining exposure of approximately $8–18K in Q1 2025, subject to matter timing.
Sources
- Gmail - 2024-11-17T18:15:00Z - gmail-professional_fees-broad - gmail-2024-11-10-ip-invoice: Invoice from Whitmore & Associates — IP dispute advisory, Matter WA-2024-IP-441. Work performed October–November in preparation for USPTO proceedings. Fee: $27,500. CFO approved 10 November.
- Slack - 2024-11-17T12:15:00Z - slack-professional_fees-broad - legal-slack-2024-11-11: Heads up — Whitmore invoice hit today, $27.5K for IP defence work. This is the large one we've been expecting. CFO approved, posted to Professional Fees. Total matter spend so far: $39.5K of ~$50K estimate.
- Gmail - 2024-11-17T18:45:00Z - gmail-professional_fees-narrow - gmail-2024-11-10-ip-background: Context from GC — Whitmore engaged in August to defend against patent claim from Vantec Corp. Expected total matter cost $45–55K over 6 months. November invoice is second billing; first $12K was absorbed within budget in September/October. Remaining $8–18K expected in Q1 2025.
- Slack - 2024-11-17T12:45:00Z - slack-professional_fees-narrow - legal-slack-2024-11-11: IP dispute context: Vantec filed patent infringement claim in September. Our outside counsel (Whitmore) assesses strong defence. USPTO proceedings begin Q1 2025. Remaining costs will be in Q1.

Legal & Compliance | +$1,200 (+24.0%) | Budget: $5,000 | Actual: $6,200
The $1,200 unfavourable variance is fully attributable to a flat-fee invoice from **Heron Legal** for the drafting and review of employment agreements for two VP-level hires: **VP Engineering** and **VP Sales**. The engagement was board-approved, the invoice was CFO-approved, and HR communications confirm the agreements were processed as part of the standard new-hire legal review process in November. This is a one-time cost linked to the executive hiring cycle and is not expected to recur unless further senior hires are made.
Sources
- Gmail - 2024-11-18T18:15:00Z - gmail-legal_&_compliance-broad - gmail-2024-11-20-exec-legal: Invoice from Heron Legal — employment agreements for two new VP hires (VP Engineering and VP Sales). $1,200 flat fee per board-approved engagement. CFO approved.
- Slack - 2024-11-18T12:15:00Z - slack-legal_&_compliance-broad - hr-slack-2024-11-20: VP Engineering and VP Sales offers accepted. Employment agreements went to Heron Legal for review this month — standard process. $1,200 total.

INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES
The following line items produced variances below the significance threshold. No investigation was performed. Recommend a brief management confirmation if any are expected to recur.
- **Audit & Accounting** — +$200 (+2.5%): Minor overage, likely timing of routine fees.
- **Software Subscriptions** — +$100 (+3.3%): Immaterial; may reflect a small price increase or rounding on renewal.
- **Office & Admin** — +$100 (+5.0%): Immaterial; consistent with normal period fluctuation.
